content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN integers. Determine if their exists a non-empty subsequence†^{\dagger}† of AAA such that the sum is divisible by 333.

Print Yes\text{Yes}Yes or No\text{No}No accordingly. You **do not** need to print an example of such a subset.

†^{\dagger}† BBB is said to be a subset of AAA if BBB can be formed by deleting some elements in AAA without rearranging the order of the remaining elements. For example, [1,2][1, 2][1,2], [1,1,2,3][1, 1, 2, 3][1,1,2,3], [2,3][2, 3][2,3] are valid subsequences of [1,1,2,3][1, 1, 2, 3][1,1,2,3] but [2,1][2, 1][2,1] or [2,2][2, 2][2,2] is not.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line Yes\text{Yes}Yes or No\text{No}No depending on whether there exists a non-empty subsequence with sum divisible by 333 or not.


Each character can be printed in either case, i.e. yes\text{yes}yes, YES\text{YES}YES and yES\text{yES}yES will all be accepted as positive responses.

sampleTestCases.output:

Yes

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There is only one element, which is not divisible by 333.
Test Case 2 : There is only one element, which is divisible by 333.
Test Case 3 : We can take the whole array [1,2][1, 2][1,2] as that has a sum of 333.
